The black shape is straddling the divider, with the left part (horizontal bar with dots) on the left side and the right part (vertical bar) on the right side. - B (black): pixels=40, bbox=[15,33]-[23,41]
After LEFT, the black shape moved left and the gray shape moved right (towards each other). Now they're overlapping at the divider.
Let me check the current segmentation to see if the gray shape is being consumed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state after LEFT
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Current nodes:")
for node in seg['nodes']:
    if node['color'] in ('B', 'c', 'Y') and node['pixels'] > 5:
        b = node['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  {node['color']}: pixels={node['pixels']}, bbox=[{min(rows)},{min(cols)}]-[{max(rows)},{max(cols)}], children={node['children']}")

# Check if gray shape is being consumed
# If so, continue LEFT to fully consume it
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
